title: Keep GenServers responsive; never block the loop
description: A GenServer processes one message at a time, so move slow or blocking work out of handle_call/handle_cast.
body: Use when a GenServer does I/O, calls external services, or runs CPU-heavy work. Do not perform a slow synchronous operation inside handle_call, since the process handles messages serially and its mailbox backs up while callers time out. Actions: offload slow work to a Task (Task.async / Task.Supervisor) and reply later, use handle_continue for post-init work, or restructure so the GenServer only guards state. Validation signals include a test that concurrent callers are served promptly and the mailbox does not grow unbounded under load. Risk boundary: a blocking handle_call serializes the whole system behind one process, causing cascading timeouts; a single GenServer is a concurrency bottleneck if it owns hot-path work it does not need to.
